criteria: Inclusion: Male or female participants (men and women).
Inclusion: Presence of early carotid atherosclerosis.
Inclusion: LDL cholesterol between the 60th and 90th percentiles (moderately elevated).
Inclusion: Men and women
Inclusion: Ventricular fibrillation (VF)
Inclusion: Ventricular tachycardia (VT) with syncope
Inclusion: Ventricular tachycardia (VT) without syncope with ejection fraction <= 0.40 and at least one of the following: systolic blood pressure < 80 mm Hg, chest pain, or near syncope
Inclusion: Men or women (sex is male or female).
Inclusion: Cigarette smoker.
Inclusion: Age between 35 and 60 years.
Inclusion: Male or female, adults or children age >= 6 months.
Inclusion: Patients with known or suspected thyroid nodules and/or thyroid cancer.
Inclusion: At-risk family members of patients who have a genetic susceptibility to developing thyroid cancer.

Exclusion: Serious underlying medical conditions that restrict diagnostic testing or therapy, such as renal failure, congestive cardiac failure, or active coexisting non-thyroid carcinoma requiring intervention before thyroid cancer is addressed.
Inclusion: Patients 2 years and older who have a disorder that, in the judgment of the treating physician, might be amenable to treatment with BTX.

Inclusion: Applicable disorders include but are not limited to dystonia, hemifacial spasm, blepharospasm, tremor, spasmodic dysphonia, tics, vocal fold tremor, oral lingual dyskinesia, tardive dyskinesia, and spasticity.
Exclusion: Women who are planning on becoming pregnant, are pregnant, or breastfeeding, for the duration of the condition.
Exclusion: Subjects who require treatment with an aminoglycoside antibiotic, until treatment is complete.
Exclusion: For laryngeal injections, subjects must have a paradoxical vocal fold movement with intermittent stridor due to either gastroesophageal reflux or emotional disorders.
Inclusion: Women of any age with clinical stage II breast cancer.
Inclusion: Primary breast tumor size less than or equal to 5 cm with clinically N0 or N1 axillary lymph nodes (T1N1, T2N0, or T2N1).
Inclusion: Patients will be staged according to the 1986 AJCC TMM classification.
Inclusion: Patients with bilateral breast cancer are eligible if at least one tumor is invasive and stage I or II, and neither breast is stage III.
Inclusion: Histologic confirmation of an invasive primary breast neoplasm of epithelial origin.
Inclusion: Geographically accessible and willing to return for follow-up at the NCI.
Inclusion: Mentally competent to understand and give informed consent.
Inclusion: Estrogen receptor status may be positive, negative, or unknown.
Inclusion: Prior cancers allowed if curatively treated with no evidence of recurrence for 10 or more years.
Exclusion: Advanced local disease or distant metastases (stage III or IV).
Exclusion: Previous therapy to the breast other than excisional biopsy.
Exclusion: Pregnancy.
Exclusion: Unwillingness to use birth control during chemotherapy.
Exclusion: Chronic disease (heart, lung, liver, kidney, blood, or metabolic disorders) that renders the patient a poor risk for surgery or chemotherapy.
Exclusion: Liver function tests exceeding 1.5 times the upper limit of normal for SGOT, SGPT, alkaline phosphatase, or total bilirubin.
Exclusion: Serum creatinine 1.7 or higher and/or creatinine clearance 45 ml/min or lower.
Exclusion: History of cardiac disease with abnormal ejection fraction on MUGA scan or presence of angina.
Inclusion: Girls and women aged 5-50 years
Inclusion: Diagnosis of Turner syndrome based on absence of all or part of one X chromosome
Inclusion: Control subjects with height and weight within ±2 standard deviations
Inclusion: Control subjects with normal intelligence
Inclusion: Control subjects with normal educational achievement
Inclusion: Biological parents (male and female) of TS subjects may be included solely to have blood drawn for genetic testing to determine the origin of their daughters' X chromosome
Exclusion: Severe physical or neurocognitive impairment preventing accurate completion of cognitive tasks
Exclusion: Normal subjects who have qualified for or participated in gifted and talented or remedial education programs
Inclusion: Diagnosis of Wegener's granulomatosis.
Inclusion: Age 10–80 years.
Inclusion: Prior documentation of vasculitis based on clinical characteristics with histopathological and/or angiographic evidence.
Inclusion: Failure to respond to prior cytotoxic therapy or toxicity from such agents in the setting of persistent disease.
Inclusion: In the absence of histopathological and/or angiographic evidence of vasculitis, positive C-ANCA (performed at the NIH).

Inclusion: In the absence of histopathological and/or angiographic evidence of vasculitis, glomerulonephritis evidenced by red blood cell casts with proteinuria or by renal biopsy showing necrotizing glomerulonephritis without immunofluorescence for immunoglobulin or complement.

Inclusion: In the absence of histopathological and/or angiographic evidence of vasculitis, inflammatory sinusitis with granulomatous histopathology and negative special stains for mycobacteria and fungi, present for at least 3 months and unresponsive to at least 2 weeks of targeted antibiotics.
Inclusion: In the absence of histopathological and/or angiographic evidence of vasculitis, pulmonary nodule or infiltrates in the absence of infection.

Inclusion: Evidence of active disease defined by Vasculitis Disease Activity Index (VDAI) >= 3, or if started on immunosuppressive therapy at an outside institution, a history of VDAI >= 3 during the past 6 months.
Exclusion: Evidence of infection by gram stain and/or culture.
Exclusion: Recent (within 4 weeks) increase in glucocorticoid or cytotoxic drug therapy.
Exclusion: Pregnant or nursing.
Exclusion: Fertile women without a negative pregnancy test within 1 week prior to study entry.
Exclusion: Fertile women not using effective birth control.
Exclusion: Acute or chronic liver disease.
Exclusion: Alcohol abuse (>14 oz of 100 proof liquor or equivalent per week).
Exclusion: Active peptic ulcer disease.
Exclusion: Inability to comply with study guidelines.
Exclusion: Serological evidence of infection with human immunodeficiency virus (HIV).
Inclusion: Children and adults from birth to 80 years of age.
Inclusion: Histologic evidence of an increased mast cell number in at least one organ system.
Inclusion: Willing to be seen at the NIH according to protocol guidelines.
Inclusion: Routine medical care available through their referral physician.
Inclusion: Patients with mastocytosis currently followed at the NIH on protocol 88-I-0190 will be continued on this study.
Inclusion: Documentation of WG based on clinical characteristics and histopathological evidence of vasculitis.
Inclusion: Positive C-ANCA or P-ANCA with glomerulonephritis evidenced by red blood cell casts and proteinuria.
Inclusion: Positive C-ANCA or P-ANCA with renal biopsy showing necrotizing glomerulonephritis without positive immunofluorescence for immunoglobulin and complement.
Inclusion: Age 10–80 years.
Inclusion: Evidence of active disease defined by a Vasculitis Disease Activity Index ≥ 3.
Inclusion: If already started on cyclophosphamide (CTX) and glucocorticoid at an outside institution, a history of Vasculitis Disease Activity Index ≥ 3 at the time therapy was initiated.
Exclusion: Evidence of active systemic infection judged by the investigator to be of greater danger than the underlying vasculitis.
Exclusion: Pregnant.
Exclusion: Nursing an infant.
Exclusion: Fertile woman without a negative pregnancy test within one week prior to study entry.
Exclusion: Fertile woman not using an effective means of birth control.
Exclusion: Acute or chronic liver disease.
Exclusion: Past history of alcohol abuse (>14 oz of 100-proof liquor or equivalent per week).
Exclusion: Ongoing alcohol use of any volume that cannot be discontinued upon study entry.
Exclusion: Serological evidence of human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Exclusion: Serological evidence of hepatitis C infection.
Exclusion: Positive hepatitis B surface antigen.
Exclusion: Inability to comply with study guidelines.

Inclusion: Has an identifiable neurological disorder or represents a diagnostic puzzle (including Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's disease, multiple system atrophy, progressive supranuclear palsy, restless legs syndrome, other cognitive or movement disorders, or diagnostic puzzles).
Inclusion: Age 18 to 90 years inclusive.
Inclusion: Suspected or established diagnosis of Marfan syndrome, Stickler syndrome, Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, or a closely related syndrome
Inclusion: Personal or family history of marfanoid body habitus suggestive of a heritable connective tissue disorder
Inclusion: Personal or family history of aortic dilatation and/or dissection suggestive of a heritable connective tissue disorder
Inclusion: Personal or family history of ectopia lentis, detached retina, vitreous degeneration, and/or early-onset high myopia suggestive of a heritable connective tissue disorder
Inclusion: Personal or family history of posterior cleft palate suggestive of a heritable connective tissue disorder
Inclusion: Personal or family history of joint laxity and/or dislocation suggestive of a heritable connective tissue disorder
Inclusion: Personal or family history of premature osteoarthritis suggestive of a heritable connective tissue disorder
Inclusion: Personal or family history of skin fragility, striae, easy bruisability, and/or hyperextensibility suggestive of a heritable connective tissue disorder
Inclusion: Personal or family history of pectus excavatum or carinatum suggestive of a heritable connective tissue disorder
Inclusion: Personal or family history of scoliosis, spondylolisthesis, and/or dural ectasia suggestive of a heritable connective tissue disorder
Inclusion: Personal or family history of high-frequency sensorineural hearing loss suggestive of a heritable connective tissue disorder
Exclusion: Inability to provide informed consent
Inclusion: Age 18 to 65 years.
Inclusion: Diagnosis of sickle cell disease with electrophoretic documentation of SS homozygosity.
Inclusion: Normal volunteer.
Inclusion: Hematocrit greater than 18% with an absolute reticulocyte count greater than 100,000/mL.
Inclusion: Hematocrit 18–21% with an absolute reticulocyte count greater than 100,000/mL (eligible with only 100 mL blood draw; may re-enter after four weeks with repeat screening labs).
Exclusion: More than two acute pain crises in the last two months.
Exclusion: Use of hydroxyurea or butyrate therapy at any time in the last 12 months.
Exclusion: Blood transfusion within the last three months.
Exclusion: Hemoglobin A percentage greater than 20%.
Exclusion: Age less than 18 years or greater than 65 years.
Exclusion: Current pregnancy or lactation.
Exclusion: Inability to exercise the anterior tibialis muscle.
Exclusion: Active cigarette smoking (inhalation of smoke from any tobacco product) in the last one month.
Exclusion: Diabetes mellitus.
Exclusion: Coronary artery disease.
Exclusion: Peripheral vascular disease.
Exclusion: Migraine headaches in the last 12 months.
Exclusion: History of previous cerebrovascular accident (CVA) or stroke.
Exclusion: Serum creatinine greater than 1.0 mg/dL.
Exclusion: Cardiac pacemaker or implantable defibrillator.
Exclusion: Aneurysm clip.
Exclusion: Neural stimulator (e.g., TENS unit).
Exclusion: Any type of ear implant.
Exclusion: Metal in the eye (e.g., from machining).
Exclusion: Any implanted device (e.g., insulin pump, drug infusion device).
Exclusion: Any metallic foreign body, shrapnel, or bullet.
Exclusion: Hematocrit less than or equal to 18%.
Exclusion: Use of aspirin, non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), or caffeine on the day of the study.
Exclusion: Current use of Viagra (sildenafil).
Inclusion: Age above 18 years.
Inclusion: Serum ALT > 41 U/L or AST > 31 U/L on the average of three determinations during the previous 6 months.
Inclusion: Presence of anti-HCV antibodies and HCV RNA in serum tested at least once during the previous 6 months.
Inclusion: Liver biopsy within the previous 12 months showing chronic hepatitis with histology activity index ≥ 6 (out of 22).
Inclusion: Written informed consent.
Exclusion: Prior interferon or ribavirin treatment with lack of sustained virological response (HCV RNA detectable 6 months after stopping therapy).
Exclusion: Prior treatment with combination alpha interferon and ribavirin.

Exclusion: Decompensated liver disease (bilirubin > 4 mg/dL, albumin < 3.0 g/dL, prothrombin time prolonged by > 2 seconds, or history of bleeding esophageal varices, ascites, or hepatic encephalopathy).
Exclusion: ALT > 1000 U/L (> 25× upper limit of normal) at enrollment.
Exclusion: Pregnancy.
Exclusion: Women of child-bearing potential or spouses of such women unable to practice adequate contraception (vasectomy, tubal ligation, condoms with spermicide, oral contraceptives, or IUD).

Exclusion: Significant systemic or major illnesses other than liver disease (e.g., congestive heart failure, renal failure with creatinine clearance < 50 mL/min, organ transplantation, serious psychiatric disease or depression, angina pectoris).
Exclusion: Pre-existing anemia (hematocrit < 36% for men or < 34% for women).
Exclusion: Known history of hemolytic anemia.
Exclusion: Antiviral or immunosuppressive therapy within the last 6 months.

Exclusion: Evidence of another liver disease in addition to viral hepatitis (e.g., autoimmune liver disease, Wilson's disease, alcoholic liver disease, hemochromatosis, alpha-1-antitrypsin deficiency).
Exclusion: Any evidence of coronary artery disease or cerebrovascular disease, including abnormal exercise stress testing in at-risk patients.
Exclusion: Active substance abuse (alcohol, inhaled or injected drugs) within the previous year.
Exclusion: Evidence of hepatocellular carcinoma (AFP > 50 ng/mL and/or imaging study demonstrating a mass suggestive of liver cancer).
Exclusion: Clinical gout.
Inclusion: Patients with known or suspected skeletal dysplasias or short stature and their family members of any age

Inclusion: Individuals and family members with a suspected or established diagnosis of any FGFR3 disorder, type II collagen disorder, Schmid metaphyseal dysplasia, pyknodysostosis, proximal symphalangism, or brachydactyly type C

Inclusion: Individuals and family members with a suspected or established diagnosis of a skeletal dysplasia in which the disease gene is not yet known (including Ellis-van Creveld, brachydactyly types B and E, and cartilage-hair hypoplasia)

Inclusion: Individuals and family members with previously uncharacterized systemic manifestations suggestive of a skeletal dysplasia, with clinical findings of disproportionate or proportionate short stature and/or cleft palate and/or detached retina and/or history of loose joints or frequent joint dislocation and/or history of, or family history of, premature arthritis
Inclusion: Individuals with generalized short stature and their family members, including adults with abnormal stature as statistically defined from standardized growth charts
Inclusion: Must be able to give informed consent
Exclusion: Older children (≥7 years old) who do not give assent
Exclusion: Persons who are wards of the state

Inclusion: Diagnosis of Wegener's granulomatosis based on clinical characteristics and histopathological and/or angiographic evidence of vasculitis or the presence of glomerulonephritis and a positive ANCA assay
Inclusion: Age between 18 and 65 years
Inclusion: No change in immunosuppressive drug therapy during the prior 4 weeks
Inclusion: Persistent disease activity (Vasculitis Activity Index ≥ 3) despite optimal therapy
Inclusion: Persistent or recurrent disease activity in a patient who cannot tolerate optimal therapy

Inclusion: Not on maximal immunosuppressive therapy but with persistent or recurrent disease activity that is not immediately threatening the function of a major organ system (in the judgment of investigators)
Inclusion: No evidence of active infection
Exclusion: Pregnant
Exclusion: Nursing (breastfeeding)
Inclusion: Fertile women must have a negative pregnancy test within one week prior to study entry
Inclusion: All participants must be using effective birth control
Inclusion: Serum creatinine < 3.5 mg/dL
Inclusion: Platelet count > 100,000/mm3
Inclusion: Leukocyte count > 3,500/mm3
Inclusion: Hemoglobin > 9 mg/dL
Inclusion: Liver function test abnormalities < 3× the upper limit of normal (serum GOT, GPT, alkaline phosphatase, and/or bilirubin)
Exclusion: Positive for anti-HIV, anti-HCV, or hepatitis B surface antigen (HBsAg)
Inclusion: Weight less than 104 kg
Exclusion: Treatment with any investigational drug within 30 days
Exclusion: Pre-existing malignancy
Exclusion: Known allergy to E. coli protein or IL-10
Exclusion: History of psychiatric illness that, in the opinion of the principal investigator, would preclude study participation
Inclusion: Clinically definite and/or laboratory-supported definite diagnosis of multiple sclerosis (MS).
Inclusion: At least 6 monthly MRI scans prior to randomization using a standardized MRI protocol.
Inclusion: Fulfill predefined MRI disease activity with a mean of at least 0.5 total gadolinium-enhancing lesions across the 6 monthly scans immediately preceding randomization.
Inclusion: No evidence of relapse for at least 30 days prior to entry.
Inclusion: One or more relapses in the 2 years preceding randomization.
Inclusion: Expanded Disability Status Scale (EDSS) score <= 7.0.
Inclusion: Age 18 to 55 years, male or female.
Inclusion: Females are post-menopausal, surgically incapable of bearing children, or using a medically accepted method of birth control.
Inclusion: Willing and able to give informed consent according to national legal requirements.
Inclusion: Age above 18 years (male or female).
Inclusion: Elevated ALT or AST averaging at least twice the upper limit of normal on three determinations taken at least one month apart during the previous six months.
Inclusion: Presence of anti-HCV and HCV RNA in serum documented at least once during the previous six months.
Inclusion: Evidence of chronic hepatitis on liver biopsy within the previous 12 months with a histology activity index of at least 6 (out of 22).

Inclusion: Contraindication to alpha interferon or prior nonresponse/intolerance, defined as any of the following: specific contraindications (depression, psychiatric illness, neurological impairment, severe thrombocytopenia, autoimmune disease); history of severe side effects or intolerance during a previous course of alpha interferon; or lack of a sustained virological response after an adequate 6-month course of alpha interferon plus ribavirin or (after September 1, 2003) peginterferon plus ribavirin.
Inclusion: Written informed consent.
Inclusion: Prior participant in protocol 98-DK-0003 who did not achieve a sustained virological response and has completed the therapy and follow-up period in that trial.
Inclusion: Prior 98-DK-0003 participant receiving ribavirin monotherapy eligible for immediate enrollment without a medication-free period.
Exclusion: Pregnancy.
Exclusion: Women of childbearing potential who are unable to practice adequate contraception.
Exclusion: Men with spouses or sexual partners of childbearing potential who are unable to practice adequate contraception.

Exclusion: Significant systemic illnesses other than liver disease, including a history of congestive heart failure, cerebral vascular disease, renal failure with creatinine clearance less than 50 ml/min, or angina pectoris.
Exclusion: Abnormal cardiac stress test or abnormal carotid ultrasound.
Exclusion: Pre-existing anemia (hematocrit less than 32%) or known history of hemolytic anemia.
Exclusion: Interferon or immunosuppressive therapy within the last 6 months.
Exclusion: Evidence of another form of liver disease in addition to viral hepatitis (e.g., autoimmune or alcoholic liver disease).
Exclusion: Active or recent (within one year) alcohol or drug abuse or psychiatric illness likely to interfere with compliance and safety monitoring.
Inclusion: Parkinsonism of undetermined etiology.
Inclusion: Age between 18 and 85 years.
Exclusion: Presence or history of any medical condition expected to subject the patient to unwarranted risk.
Exclusion: History of convulsive disorders.
Exclusion: History of hepatic disorders.
Exclusion: History of renal disorders.
Exclusion: Currently receiving treatment for myocardial infarction.
Exclusion: Currently receiving treatment for arrhythmia.
Exclusion: Currently receiving treatment for cardiac failure.
Exclusion: Pregnant.
Exclusion: Not practicing an effective means of birth control.
Inclusion: Diagnosis of idiopathic Parkinson's disease based on characteristic clinical history and neurologic findings
Inclusion: Age between 18 and 75 years
Inclusion: Male or female sex
Exclusion: Any presence or history of a medical condition that could reasonably be expected to subject the patient to unwarranted risk
Exclusion: Baseline QTc prolongation greater than 440 msec
Exclusion: Pregnant women
Exclusion: Individuals not practicing effective birth control
Inclusion: Clinical diagnosis of Parkinson's disease based on characteristic clinical history and neurological findings.
Inclusion: Hoehn & Yahr stage II-IV.
Inclusion: Male or female aged 30-76 years.
Exclusion: Any medical condition (present or historical) that could reasonably be expected to subject the patient to unwarranted risk.
Exclusion: History of significant cardiac disease, including myocardial infarction within 12 months prior to study, dysrhythmia, or QTc interval greater than 440 msec.
Exclusion: Convulsive (seizure) disorder.
Exclusion: Hepatic disorder with liver function tests above the upper limit of normal.
Exclusion: Renal disorder with creatinine above the upper limit of normal.
Exclusion: Evidence of other serious medical illness.
Exclusion: History of alcohol or drug abuse.
Exclusion: Participation in an investigational trial within 28 days prior to study.
Exclusion: Pregnant women.
Exclusion: Nursing (breastfeeding) women.
Exclusion: Not practicing effective birth control.
Inclusion: Age 18 years or older
Inclusion: Referred for care
Inclusion: Patients with chronic hepatitis C
Inclusion: Age 18 to 70 years, male or female
Inclusion: HCV infection indicated by the presence of anti-HCV by ELISA and recombinant immunoblot assay (RIBA)
Inclusion: Written informed consent
Exclusion: Women who are pregnant
Exclusion: Significant systemic illnesses other than liver disease, including congestive heart failure, renal failure, or uncontrolled diabetes mellitus
Exclusion: Antiviral or immunosuppressive therapy within the last 6 months
Exclusion: HIV infection

Exclusion: Contraindications to liver biopsy, such as clotting abnormality (e.g., coumadin therapy, platelet count less than 50,000/cubic millimeter, or prothrombin time more than 4 seconds beyond control)
Inclusion: Positive AFB smear and/or culture for Mycobacterium tuberculosis, M. avium, or other mycobacterial infection.
Inclusion: For HIV-positive patients, CD4 count < 500 cells/mm3.
Inclusion: For HIV-positive patients, currently receiving antiretroviral therapy.
Inclusion: At least one of the following must be present: Temperature over 38 C on at least two occasions in the week prior to study entry.
Inclusion: At least one of the following must be present: Recent weight loss of more than 5 kilograms.

Inclusion: At least one of the following must be present: Pulmonary involvement of one or more lobes or involvement of other tissues due to tuberculosis or other mycobacterial infections, or symptomatic infections related to HIV status.
Inclusion: At least one of the following must be present: Night sweats on two or more occasions in the week prior to study entry.
Inclusion: Hospitalized men aged 18-65 years.
Inclusion: Hospitalized postmenopausal women up to age 65 years.
Inclusion: Anticipated requirement for hospitalization of at least 10 days.
Exclusion: Neuropathy or other disorders with risk of neuropathy.
Inclusion: Newly diagnosed undifferentiated nonlymphoblastic non-Hodgkin's lymphoma (Burkitt's or non-Burkitt's)
Inclusion: Newly diagnosed large cell lymphoma with CNS involvement
Inclusion: Recurrent non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
Inclusion: L-3 leukemia included
Inclusion: B-cell leukemia included

Inclusion: Must meet at least one of the following: LDH at least 500 IU/L (old method) or at least 2,000 IU/L (new method); bone marrow involvement >5% lymphoblasts; or CNS involvement evidenced by lymphoblasts on CSF cytospin or intracranial mass on CT or MRI
Inclusion: Age any age
Inclusion: Prior therapy allowed for non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
Exclusion: Any prior therapy for diseases other than non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
Inclusion: Histologically proven invasive carcinoma of the cervix
Inclusion: Previously untreated cervical cancer
Inclusion: Histology is squamous cell carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, or adenosquamous carcinoma
Inclusion: Bulky stage IB cervical cancer
Inclusion: Exophytic lesion at least 4 cm in diameter or cervix expanded to at least 4 cm presumed due to cancer
Inclusion: No extension beyond the cervix clinically and by IVP or CT with contrast
Inclusion: Performance status GOG 0-2
Inclusion: WBC at least 3,000/mm3
Inclusion: Platelet count at least 100,000/mm3
Inclusion: Bilirubin no greater than 1.5 times normal
Inclusion: AST no greater than 3 times normal
Inclusion: Alkaline phosphatase no greater than 3 times normal
Inclusion: Creatinine no greater than 2.0 mg/dL
Exclusion: Septicemia or severe infection
Exclusion: Other concurrent malignancy within the past 5 years, except curatively treated nonmelanomatous skin cancer
Inclusion: Prior malignancy therapy must not contraindicate current protocol therapy
Inclusion: Suitable for radical hysterectomy and lymphadenectomy
Exclusion: Prior therapy for cervical cancer
Exclusion: Prior pelvic irradiation
Inclusion: Histologically confirmed adenocarcinoma of the breast with at least one positive axillary lymph node

Inclusion: Adenocarcinoma of the breast with negative axillary nodes but high risk for recurrence, defined as tumor size at least 1 cm and either poorly differentiated (SBR grade III or MSBR grade V) and/or lymphatic/vascular invasion
Inclusion: Complete surgical removal of the breast plus axillary node dissection
Inclusion: Partial surgical removal of the breast plus axillary node dissection with planned breast irradiation following completion of adjuvant chemotherapy
Inclusion: No evidence of residual tumor in the axilla after axillary dissection
Inclusion: No microscopic residual tumor at the resection margins following total mastectomy

Inclusion: For partial mastectomy with microscopic residual disease at the margins, either further excision is performed or, if not, a radiotherapy boost to the tumor bed is given in addition to post-chemotherapy breast irradiation
Inclusion: Preoperative clinical stage T1–T3a, N0–2, M0
Exclusion: Clinical T4 disease (extension to the chest wall, edema including peau d'orange, skin ulceration, satellite skin nodules confined to the same breast, or inflammatory carcinoma)
Inclusion: Postoperative pathologic stage TNM I–III (T0–4; N0–2; M0), with T4 allowed only when limited to dermal involvement on pathology

Inclusion: No evidence of metastatic disease beyond the ipsilateral axillary nodes on pre-chemotherapy chest x-ray and bone scan (with radiographs of suspicious areas), and abdominal ultrasound if liver tests are elevated
Inclusion: Simultaneous bilateral breast carcinoma permitted only if complete tumor resection has been performed on both breasts
Inclusion: If both breasts are clinically node-positive, axillary dissection on both sides must meet the same criteria as above
Inclusion: Adjuvant chemotherapy must begin within 14 weeks of initial pathologic diagnosis
Inclusion: Hormone receptor status of any level is allowed; if assessed by biochemical assay, numeric values must be available
Inclusion: Female sex

Inclusion: Pre- or perimenopausal status, defined by at least one of the following: normal menstruation; amenorrhea for less than 1 year (up to 3 years if under age 52); biochemical evidence of ovarian function; hysterectomy without bilateral oophorectomy in patients under age 56; or premenopausal women age 50 or younger who began hormone replacement therapy before amenorrhea
Inclusion: ECOG performance status 0–2 prior to chemotherapy
Inclusion: WBC ≥ 3,000/mm3
Inclusion: Polymorphs plus bands (neutrophils) ≥ 1,500/mm3
Inclusion: Platelet count ≥ 100,000/mm3
Inclusion: Alkaline phosphatase ≤ 2 × upper limit of normal (unless abdominal ultrasound indicates liver metastasis)
Inclusion: AST and/or ALT ≤ 2 × upper limit of normal (unless abdominal ultrasound indicates liver metastasis)
Exclusion: History of serious underlying medical illness or psychiatric or addictive disorder
Exclusion: Second malignancy within 5 years, except curatively treated nonmelanoma skin cancer, or curatively treated endometrial, colon, or thyroid cancer, or carcinoma in situ of the cervix
Exclusion: Planned pregnancy during the 5-year study period
Inclusion: Fertile women must use effective contraception other than oral contraception
Inclusion: Patient is accessible for treatment and follow-up
Exclusion: Prior chemotherapy
Exclusion: Concurrent cytotoxic therapy other than protocol therapy
Inclusion: If receiving adjuvant tamoxifen up to 2 weeks before or during adjuvant chemotherapy, it must be discontinued at randomization
Exclusion: Long-term prednisone or other hormones
Inclusion: Newly diagnosed, active multiple myeloma of any stage requiring treatment
Inclusion: Smoldering myeloma (Durie-Salmon stage I) eligible only with a 25% or greater increase in M component levels and/or Bence-Jones protein excretion or development of symptoms
Inclusion: Quantifiable M component of IgG, IgA, IgD, IgE, and/or urinary kappa or lambda light chain (Bence-Jones protein) excretion required
Inclusion: For non-secretory disease or secretory disease without quantifiable protein, plasmacytosis of at least 30% is acceptable
Exclusion: IgM peaks
Inclusion: For patients 55 years of age and younger, evaluation of siblings as potential allogeneic bone marrow transplant donors required
Inclusion: HLA followed by DR and MLC testing required for donor evaluation
Inclusion: Patients with renal failure (even on dialysis) are eligible if the cause is attributed to myeloma (Bence-Jones protein or hypercalcemia)
Inclusion: Patients with renal failure are eligible only if the duration does not exceed 2 months
Inclusion: If medically appropriate, pathologic fractures should be treated prior to registration
Inclusion: If medically appropriate, pneumonia at diagnosis should be treated prior to registration
Inclusion: If medically appropriate, hyperviscosity with shortness of breath should be treated prior to registration
Inclusion: Age 70 years and under
Inclusion: SWOG performance status 0-2
Inclusion: SWOG performance status 3 or 4 allowed only if solely due to bone pain
Inclusion: Normal ejection fraction by ECHO or MUGA
Exclusion: Myocardial infarction within 6 months
Exclusion: Unstable angina
Exclusion: Difficult to control congestive heart failure
Exclusion: Uncontrolled hypertension
Exclusion: Difficult to control arrhythmias
Exclusion: History of chronic cerebrovascular accident
Exclusion: History of chronic obstructive or restrictive pulmonary disease
Inclusion: Pulmonary function studies and DLCO at least 50% of predicted, unless decreased due to myeloma involvement
Inclusion: Exception to pulmonary function requirement allowed if myeloma involvement is demonstrated on bronchoscopy and/or open lung biopsy
Exclusion: Uncontrolled diabetes
Exclusion: Significant comorbid medical condition
Exclusion: Uncontrolled, life-threatening infection
Exclusion: Prior malignancy within 5 years, except adequately treated nonmelanoma skin cancer or carcinoma in situ of the cervix
Exclusion: Prior malignancy treated with cytotoxic drugs used on this protocol
Exclusion: Pregnant or nursing
Inclusion: Fertile patients must use effective contraception
Exclusion: Prior chemotherapy

Exclusion: Prior radiotherapy, unless local and the cumulative prior dose plus potential protocol TBI does not exceed: less than 5,000 cGy to bone; less than 4,000 cGy to mediastinum, heart, small bowel, brain, and spinal cord; less than 2,000 cGy to the liver; and less than 1,500 cGy to the kidneys and lungs
Inclusion: Multiple myeloma confirmed by bone marrow plasmacytosis.
Inclusion: Measurable M-component in the serum or urine by immunoelectrophoresis or immunofixation.
Exclusion: Stage I myeloma.
Exclusion: Smoldering multiple myeloma.

Inclusion: Refractory to or in first relapse following an initial response to VAD (vincristine/doxorubicin/dexamethasone), with relapse defined as any of the following: 50% increase above the lowest remission level of serum or urine M-protein while on therapy; or a 25-50% increase above the lowest remission level of serum or urine M-protein associated with hypercalcemia (>11 mg/dl), hemoglobin decrease of 2 g/dl attributable to increasing marrow plasmacytosis, or appearance of new lytic lesions.
Inclusion: Serum calcium no greater than 11 mg/dl.
Exclusion: Myeloma meningitis.
Exclusion: Plasma cell leukemia.
Inclusion: Age 18 to 65 years.
Inclusion: ECOG performance status 0-2.
Inclusion: WBC greater than 500 (after G-CSF).
Inclusion: Platelets greater than 25,000.
Inclusion: Total bilirubin no greater than 2.0 mg/dl.
Inclusion: Serum creatinine no greater than 2.0 mg/dl.
Exclusion: New York Heart Association (NYHA) class II-IV cardiovascular disease.
Inclusion: DLCO at least 50% of predicted.
Inclusion: FVC at least 75% of predicted.
Inclusion: FEV1 at least 60% of predicted.
Exclusion: Uncontrolled infection.
Exclusion: Active fungal infection.
Exclusion: Fever.
Exclusion: Prior malignancy within 5 years, except basal cell skin cancer or in situ carcinoma of the cervix.
Exclusion: Pregnant or nursing women.
Inclusion: Prior biological response modifiers allowed.
Inclusion: No time limit between prior cytotoxic chemotherapy and protocol treatment.
Inclusion: Prior cumulative melphalan dose less than 300 mg.
Inclusion: Corticosteroids for hypercalcemia allowed.
Inclusion: Prior radiotherapy allowed.
Inclusion: Prior pelvic radiotherapy allowed.
Inclusion: Pathologically documented EBV antigen–positive lymphoproliferative disease, lymphoma, or other EBV-associated malignancy
Inclusion: Severely immunocompromised patients with blood EBV DNA levels >500 copies/mL (high risk for EBV lymphoproliferative disease)
Inclusion: Patients developing or at risk for EBV lymphomas or lymphoproliferative disorders following an allogeneic marrow transplant
Inclusion: Patients developing or at risk for EBV lymphomas or lymphoproliferative disorders following an allogeneic organ transplant
Inclusion: Patients with AIDS developing EBV lymphomas or lymphoproliferative diseases due to profound HIV-induced immunodeficiency

Inclusion: Target ICA vessel diameter visually estimated within device-specific ranges: > 2.5 mm and < 7.0 mm for Emboshield Pro or Emboshield NAV6; > 2.8 mm and < 6.2 mm for Emboshield Gen 3; and > 4.0 mm and < 9.0 mm for the Xact stent treatment segment; contralateral ICA may be used for estimation if the target is too stenosed.

Inclusion: FEV1 < 30% predicted.
Inclusion: Dialysis-dependent renal failure.
Inclusion: Uncontrolled diabetes defined as fasting glucose > 400 mg/dl and ketones > 2+.
Inclusion: Restenosis after previous carotid endarterectomy (>= 50% by angiography for symptomatic patients or >= 80% by angiography for asymptomatic patients).
Inclusion: Status post radiation treatment to the neck.
Inclusion: Status post radical neck surgery.
Inclusion: Surgically inaccessible lesions (e.g., lesions above the level of C2 or below the clavicle, lesions obstructed by tumors in the neck).
Inclusion: Spinal immobility (inability to flex neck beyond neutral) or kyphotic deformity.
Inclusion: Presence of tracheostomy stoma.
Inclusion: Contralateral laryngeal nerve paralysis.
Inclusion: Target lesion is a discrete lesion located in the ICA (with or without involvement of the contiguous common carotid artery).
Inclusion: Carotid stenosis is >= 50% by angiography for symptomatic patients or >= 80% by angiography for asymptomatic patients.
Inclusion: Target ICA vessel reference diameter is >= 4.0 mm and <= 9.0 mm by angiography.

Inclusion: For ACCUNET System use, there is expected ability to deliver the ACCUNET distal to the lesion (absence of excessive tortuosity) and an available straight or mildly angulated segment >= 4 cm in the distal ICA (prior to the petrous portion) to place the embolic protection device.
Inclusion: For ACCUNET System use, the diameter of the straight or mildly angulated distal ICA segment (prior to the petrous portion) is >= 3.25 mm and <= 7.5 mm by angiography.
Inclusion: If bilateral carotid stenosis is present and the non-study artery will be treated, treatment of the non-study artery must occur > 30 days before or > 30 days after the study procedure.
Exclusion: Evolving stroke.
Exclusion: History of intolerance or allergic reaction to any study medications or materials, including heparin, aspirin, baby aspirin, nitinol, or x-ray contrast.
Exclusion: History of intolerance or allergic reaction to both ticlopidine and clopidogrel.
Exclusion: Active bleeding diathesis or coagulopathy, or refusal of blood transfusions.
Exclusion: History of major ipsilateral stroke likely to confound study endpoints.
Exclusion: Severe dementia.
Exclusion: History of spontaneous intracranial hemorrhage within the past 12 months.
Exclusion: Recent (< 7 days) stroke of sufficient size (on CT or MRI) to place the patient at risk of hemorrhagic conversion during the procedure.
Exclusion: Hemorrhagic transformation of an ischemic stroke within the past 60 days.
Exclusion: Hemoglobin < 8 g/dl (unless on dialysis), platelet count < 50,000, uncorrected INR > 1.5, or heparin-associated thrombocytopenia.
Exclusion: Any condition that precludes proper angiographic assessment or makes percutaneous arterial access unsafe (e.g., morbid obesity, sustained systolic blood pressure > 180 mm Hg).

Exclusion: Neurologic illness within the past two years characterized by fleeting or fixed neurologic deficit indistinguishable from TIA or stroke (e.g., seizures, complicated/classic migraine, tumor or other space-occupying brain lesions, subdural hematoma, cerebral contusion or other post-traumatic lesions, intracranial infection, demyelinating disease, moderate to severe dementia, or intracranial hemorrhage).

Exclusion: Active participation in another drug or device trial (IND or IDE) without completed required protocol follow-up, prior enrollment in ARCHeR, or participation in another trial during ARCHeR follow-up.
Exclusion: Patient or legally authorized representative is unable to understand and cooperate with study procedures or provide informed consent.
Exclusion: Life expectancy less than two years.
Exclusion: Vertebrobasilar insufficiency symptoms only, without clearly identifiable symptoms referable to the targeted carotid artery.

Exclusion: Known cardiac sources of emboli (e.g., left ventricular aneurysm, atrial fibrillation, intracardiac filling defect, cardiomyopathy, aortic or mitral prosthetic heart valve, cardioversion of atrial fibrillation within 30 days prior to intervention, calcific aortic stenosis, endocarditis, mitral stenosis, atrial septal defect, atrial septal aneurysm, or left atrial myxoma).
Exclusion: Recent gastrointestinal bleed that would interfere with antiplatelet therapy.
Exclusion: Severe vascular tortuosity or anatomy that would preclude safe introduction of a guide catheter, guide sheath, embolic protection system, or stent system.
Exclusion: Presence of a previously placed intravascular stent or intravascular graft in the ipsilateral distribution (patch grafts are not an exclusion).
Exclusion: Extensive or diffuse atherosclerotic disease involving the aortic arch and proximal common carotid artery that would preclude safe introduction of a guide catheter or guide sheath.
Exclusion: Intraluminal filling defect (endoluminal lucency surrounded by contrast in multiple projections, without angiographic calcification) not associated with an ulcerated target lesion.

Exclusion: Other abnormal angiographic findings such as ipsilateral intracranial or extracranial arterial stenosis greater than the lesion to be treated, cerebral aneurysm >= 5 mm, cerebral AVM, AV fistula, or other confounding intracranial lesion.
Exclusion: Bilateral carotid stenosis if the intervention is planned within the 30-day ARCHeR periprocedure period.
Exclusion: Occlusion (TIMI 0) or "string sign" > 1 cm of the ipsilateral common or internal carotid artery.

Exclusion: Have participated in an interventional medical, surgical, or pharmaceutical study within 30 days of screening, including use of investigational drugs not yet approved for any indication.
Exclusion: Receiving beta blockers.
Exclusion: Receiving treatment with a drug directly affecting gastrointestinal motility (e.g., metoclopramide, cisapride, or chronic macrolide antibiotics).

Exclusion: Have received systemic glucocorticoid therapy by oral, intravenous, or intramuscular route within 6 weeks of screening, or are regularly treated with potent inhaled/intranasal steroids with high systemic absorption or bronchodilators.

Exclusion: Have been treated with drugs that promote weight loss (e.g., phentermine, rimonabant, orlistat, sibutramine, phenylpropanolamine, or similar over-the-counter medications) within 3 months of screening.

Exclusion: Have been treated for longer than 2 weeks with any of the following within 3 months prior to screening: insulin, alpha-glucosidase inhibitors, meglitinides, sulfonylureas, or DPP-IV inhibitors.
Exclusion: Have donated blood within 60 days of screening.
Inclusion: Previously diagnosed with multiple myeloma; Durie-Salmon Stage I, II, or III based on standard criteria.

Inclusion: Progressive disease as defined by any of the following: ≥25% increase in serum M-protein or Bence Jones protein with an absolute increase of ≥0.5 g/dL serum M-protein or ≥200 mg/24 h urine light chain; for non-secretory disease, >25% increase in marrow plasma cells or an absolute increase of ≥10%; or development/worsening of lytic bone lesions or soft tissue plasmacytomas, hypercalcemia, or relapse from complete response.
Inclusion: Age ≥18 years and willing and able to comply with protocol requirements.
Inclusion: Signed informed consent.
Inclusion: Females who are not post-menopausal or surgically sterilized must use an acceptable method of birth control for the duration of the study.
Inclusion: Males must agree to use an acceptable method of contraception for the duration of the study.
Inclusion: ECOG performance status ≤2.
Inclusion: Life expectancy ≥3 months.
Inclusion: Baseline labs within 14 days of Cycle 1 Day 1: ANC >1,000/µL without colony-stimulating factors.
Inclusion: Baseline labs within 14 days of Cycle 1 Day 1: Platelets >50,000/µL without transfusion support for 7 days before the test.
Inclusion: Baseline labs within 14 days of Cycle 1 Day 1: Total bilirubin ≤2.0 mg/dL.
Inclusion: Baseline labs within 14 days of Cycle 1 Day 1: AST ≤4 × upper limit of normal.
Inclusion: Renal insufficiency, including dialysis dependence, is permitted.
Inclusion: At least 2 prior therapeutic regimens for treatment of multiple myeloma.

Inclusion: Prior nonmyeloablative transplantation allowed if no significant graft-versus-host disease and off aggressive immunosuppressive therapy for ≥30 days; low-dose immunosuppression allowed (e.g., prednisone ≤10 mg/day, low-dose tacrolimus or equivalent).
Inclusion: Mobilization with chemotherapy followed by single or tandem autologous transplantation counts as 1 prior regimen.
Inclusion: Mobilization with chemotherapy followed by autologous and subsequent nonmyeloablative allogeneic transplantation counts as 1 prior regimen.
Inclusion: Any combination of drugs given concurrently counts as a single prior regimen.
Exclusion: Pregnant or breastfeeding.
Exclusion: History of allergic reaction to compounds containing boron or mannitol.
Exclusion: Active uncontrolled viral (including HIV), bacterial, or fungal infection.
Exclusion: Grade III or IV toxicity due to previous anti-neoplastic therapy (except alopecia).
Exclusion: Grade ≥2 motor or sensory neuropathy per NCI Common Toxicity Criteria.
Exclusion: Myocardial infarction within 6 months of enrollment.
Exclusion: New York Heart Association Class III or IV heart failure.
Exclusion: Uncontrolled angina.
Exclusion: Severe uncontrolled arrhythmias.
Exclusion: Electrocardiographic evidence of acute ischemia.

Exclusion: LVEF <35% by MUGA (if MUGA is performed).

Exclusion: Less than 3 weeks since most recent chemotherapy or concurrent chemotherapy.
Exclusion: Use of corticosteroids at >10 mg prednisone/day (or equivalent).
Inclusion: diagnosis of Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection
Exclusion: all other granulomatous or neoplastic diseases

Exclusion: Any current illness requiring daily medication, except for vitamins, contraceptives, topical medications, antihistamines, antacids and other reflux medications, smoking cessation medications, headache medications without antipyretic activity, nasal allergy medications, ophthalmologic and otic medications, psychiatric medications for well-controlled conditions (e.g., depression or anxiety), and thyroxine for stable, inactive hypothyroidism; other medications require approval from the PI, sponsor, and medical monitor.
Exclusion: Daily use of oral, nasal, inhaled, or parenteral steroids or non-steroidal anti-inflammatory medications.
Exclusion: Any use of antibiotics in the 7 days before enrollment.
Exclusion: Vital signs that are abnormal and thought by the investigator to increase risk (mild elevations or depressions of blood pressure or heart rate may be allowed).
Exclusion: Current alcohol or illicit drug dependence.
Exclusion: History of receipt of an influenza vaccine in the 2008–2009 and/or 2009–2010 season.
Exclusion: History of Guillain-Barré Syndrome.
Exclusion: Failure to pass the written exam (passing score is 70% or greater).
Exclusion: Receipt of an experimental agent (e.g., vaccine or medication) within the 30 days prior to enrollment or expectation of receipt while participating in this study.
Exclusion: Receipt of a live, attenuated vaccine within the 30 days prior to enrollment.
Exclusion: Receipt of a subunit or killed vaccine within the 14 days prior to enrollment.
Exclusion: Receipt of a blood product, including immunoglobulin, in the 90 days prior to enrollment.

Exclusion: Oral temperature greater than 37.7 degrees C or other acute illness occurring prior to inoculation on the day of vaccination (will lead to postponement of enrollment; mild resolving illnesses may be allowed at investigator discretion).
Exclusion: Any condition that, in the opinion of the investigator, will compromise the volunteer’s participation with respect to rights or risks.
Inclusion: Subject is ≥ 6 months to < 5 years of age or 18 years of age or older on the day of inclusion.

Inclusion: Parent/legal guardian (for subjects ≥ 6 months to < 5 years) or adult subject (if 18 years or older) is willing and able to attend scheduled visits and comply with study procedures for the entire study duration.
Inclusion: Subject is in reasonably good health as assessed by the Investigator.
Inclusion: Parent/legal guardian (for subjects ≥ 6 months to < 5 years) or adult subject (if 18 years or older) is willing and able to give informed consent.
Inclusion: For subjects ≥ 6 months to < 5 years, born at full term (≥ 37 weeks) with a birth weight ≥ 2.5 kg (5.5 lbs).
Inclusion: For a woman, inability to bear a child or a negative serum/urine pregnancy test, if applicable.
Exclusion: History of allergy to egg proteins, chicken proteins, or constituents of the vaccine (e.g., thimerosal or formaldehyde).
Exclusion: History of serious adverse reaction to any influenza vaccine.
Exclusion: Laboratory-confirmed influenza infection or influenza vaccination in the six months preceding enrollment.
Exclusion: Any vaccination scheduled between Visit 1 and Visit 2 or Visit 3.
Exclusion: Participation in any other interventional drug or vaccine trial during participation in this study.
Exclusion: Thrombocytopenia or a bleeding disorder contraindicating intramuscular vaccination.
Exclusion: Any condition that, in the opinion of the Investigator, would pose a health risk if enrolled or could interfere with evaluation of the vaccine.
Exclusion: Prior personal history of Guillain-Barré syndrome.
Exclusion: For subjects > 6 months to < 5 years, known or suspected impairment of immunologic function or receipt of immunosuppressive therapy or immunoglobulin since birth.
Exclusion: For subjects > 6 months to < 5 years, personal or immediate family history of congenital immune deficiency.
Exclusion: For subjects > 6 months to < 5 years, developmental delay, neurologic disorder, or seizure disorder.
Exclusion: For subjects > 6 months to < 5 years, chronic medical, congenital, or developmental disorder.
Exclusion: For subjects > 6 months to < 5 years, known HIV-positive mother.

Exclusion: For subjects 18 years and older, immunocompromising condition; immunosuppressive therapy (including systemic steroid use for two weeks or more); or cancer chemotherapy or radiation therapy at enrollment, planned during the study, or within the past six months.
Exclusion: For subjects 18 years and older, diabetes mellitus requiring pharmacological control.
Exclusion: For subjects 18 years and older, person deprived of freedom by an administrative or court order (having legal or medical guardian).

Exclusion: For women of childbearing potential, a positive urine pregnancy test, breastfeeding, or not using a medically approved and reliable form of contraception (oral contraceptives or double barrier method) for the duration of the trial.
Exclusion: For subjects 18 years and older, current use of alcohol or recreational drugs that may interfere with the ability to comply with trial procedures.
Exclusion: For subjects 18 years and older, an acute illness with or without fever in the 72 hours preceding enrollment in the trial.
Exclusion: For subjects 18 years and older, clinically significant findings in vital signs or review of systems (per Investigator judgment).
Exclusion: For subjects 18 years and older, receipt of any vaccinations within the preceding 14 days.
Exclusion: For subjects 18 years and older, participation in any other interventional clinical trial within 30 days prior to enrollment.
Exclusion: For subjects 18 years and older, receipt of blood or blood products within the three months preceding enrollment.
Inclusion: Japanese male or female adults aged 20 to 64 years (inclusive) at the time of first vaccination.
Inclusion: In good general health as assessed by medical history and physical examination.
Inclusion: Investigator believes the subject can and will comply with protocol requirements.
Inclusion: Written informed consent obtained from the subject.
Inclusion: Access to a consistent means of telephone contact (home or workplace; landline or mobile; not a pay phone or other multiple-user device).
Inclusion: Female of non-childbearing potential may be enrolled.
Inclusion: Female of childbearing potential has practiced adequate contraception for 30 days prior to vaccination.
Inclusion: Female of childbearing potential has a negative pregnancy test on the day of vaccination.
Inclusion: Female of childbearing potential agrees to continue adequate contraception during the entire treatment period and for 2 months after completion of the vaccination series.
Exclusion: Use of any investigational or non-registered product (other than the study vaccine[s]) within 30 days before the first dose or planned use during the study period.
Exclusion: History of previous administration of a novel [H1N1]v vaccine.
Exclusion: Previous participation in study NCT00742885.
Exclusion: Significant acute or chronic, uncontrolled medical or psychiatric illness.
Exclusion: Substance abuse or neurological/psychiatric diagnoses that, even if stable, are deemed by the investigator to impair accurate safety reporting.
Exclusion: Axillary temperature >= 37.5 °C, or acute symptoms greater than mild severity on the scheduled date of first vaccination.
Exclusion: Diagnosed with cancer or treated for cancer within the past 3 years.
Exclusion: Confirmed or suspected immunosuppressive or immunodeficient condition, including history of HIV infection.
Exclusion: Chronic administration of immunosuppressants or other immune-modifying drugs within 6 months before enrolment or planned during the study period.
Exclusion: Receipt of immunoglobulins and/or blood products within 3 months before enrolment or planned during the study period.
Exclusion: Significant coagulation disorder or treatment with warfarin derivatives or heparin.
Exclusion: Acute evolving neurological disorder or history of Guillain-Barré syndrome.

Exclusion: Administration of any vaccines (except seasonal influenza vaccine) within 30 days before vaccination, or planned administration between the first vaccination and Day 42 blood sampling, or within 30 days prior to Day 182 blood sampling.

Exclusion: Administration of any seasonal influenza vaccine within 14 days before Day 0 vaccination, or planned administration between the first vaccination and Day 42 blood sampling, or within 14 days prior to Day 182 blood sampling.

Exclusion: Known or suspected allergy to any constituent of influenza vaccines or to components used in manufacturing the study vaccine; history of anaphylactic-type reaction to any constituent of influenza vaccines; or history of severe adverse reaction to a previous influenza vaccine.
Exclusion: Known pregnancy or a positive urine pregnancy test prior to the first vaccination.
Exclusion: Lactating or nursing female.
Exclusion: Body mass index (BMI) < 18.5 or BMI >= 30.
Exclusion: Any condition that, in the opinion of the investigator, prevents participation in the study.
Exclusion: Clinically or virologically confirmed influenza infection within 6 months preceding study start.
Inclusion: Patient has chest wall deformity, neuromuscular disease, or obesity hypoventilation syndrome with FEV1/FVC > 70% and VC < 50% predicted
Inclusion: Patient has COPD with FEV1/FVC < 70% and FEV1 < 50% predicted
Inclusion: pH > 7.35
Inclusion: Epworth Sleepiness Scale (ESS) < 18
Inclusion: Symptomatically stable with clinical resolution of intercurrent infection, with normal C-reactive protein, normal white cell count, and afebrile
Inclusion: Daytime symptoms compatible with nocturnal hypoventilation (e.g., poor sleep, morning headache, daytime somnolence, or shortness of breath)
Inclusion: Arterial PaCO2 > 6.0 kPa during the day with evidence of nocturnal hypoventilation (TcCO2 > 7.5 kPa or a rise in TcCO2 > 1 kPa)
Inclusion: No prior domiciliary ventilation use
Inclusion: For COPD patients, established on optimal medical treatment prior to enrolment
Exclusion: Psychological, social, or geographical situation that would impair compliance with the schedule
Exclusion: Underlying malignancy
Exclusion: Severe cardiac dysfunction (ejection fraction < 40%)
Exclusion: Complex obstructive sleep apnea (complex OSA)
Inclusion: ECOG performance status 0, 1, or 2
Inclusion: Diagnosis of symptomatic multiple myeloma

Inclusion: High-risk multiple myeloma defined by progressive disease <= 12 months after high-dose chemotherapy and autologous HSC transplant or by poor prognostic features (deletion of chromosome 13 or hypodiploidy by standard cytogenetics, t(4;14) by FISH, t(14;16) by FISH, 17p- by FISH, or plasma cell labeling index >= 3%)

Inclusion: Availability of an HLA fully matched or 1-mismatch related donor by low-resolution typing for A, B, C, DRB1, DQB1, or an HLA fully matched unrelated donor by high-resolution typing for A, B, C, DRB1 and at least low-resolution for DQB1
Inclusion: Recovery from toxicity of previous chemotherapy (persistent grade 1 neurotoxicity and hematological toxicity permitted)
Inclusion: Physically and psychologically capable of undergoing bone marrow or peripheral blood stem cell transplant
Inclusion: Voluntary written informed consent prior to any study-related procedure not part of normal medical care, with the option to withdraw at any time
Inclusion: Female is post-menopausal or surgically sterilized or willing to use an acceptable method of birth control for the duration of the study
Inclusion: Male agrees to use an acceptable method of contraception for the duration of the study

Exclusion: Myocardial infarction within 6 months prior to enrollment or NYHA Class III or IV heart failure, uncontrolled angina, severe uncontrolled ventricular arrhythmias, or ECG evidence of acute ischemia or active conduction system abnormalities
Exclusion: Any ECG abnormality at screening must be documented by the investigator as not medically relevant prior to study entry
Exclusion: Significant cardiac dysfunction defined as left ventricular ejection fraction < 40% or presence of symptomatic coronary artery disease
Exclusion: Significant pulmonary disease defined as FEV < 50% or CLCO < 50% of predicted values
Exclusion: Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy grade > 1
Exclusion: Significant renal dysfunction defined as estimated creatinine clearance < 50 ml/min
Exclusion: Significant liver dysfunction defined as total bilirubin >= 2 x ULN or AST/ALT >= 3 x ULN
Exclusion: Seroreactive for HIV, HTLV I or II, HBV, or HCV
Exclusion: Presence of uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infection
Exclusion: Known allergy to any component of the investigational treatment regimen or required ancillary treatments
Exclusion: Considered unable to tolerate the included doses of total body irradiation due to previous radiation treatment
Exclusion: Female is pregnant or breast-feeding
Exclusion: Other active concurrent malignancy
Exclusion: Prior allogeneic bone marrow or peripheral blood stem cell transplant
Exclusion: Received other investigational drugs <= 14 days prior to enrollment
Inclusion: Depressive symptoms of mild intensity as defined by ICD-10 codes F32.0 (mild depressive episode) or F33.0 (recurrent depressive disorder, current episode mild).
Inclusion: SDS Index between 51 and 59 (criterion declared null and void on 21.02.02 in the 2nd Amendment).
Inclusion: HAM-D21 score between 10 and 17.
Inclusion: Age between 18 and 70 years.
Inclusion: Duration of depression not less than 4 weeks and not longer than 2 years.
Inclusion: Signed informed consent to participate after verbal and written information.
Inclusion: Baseline health is stable.
Inclusion: Has a clinical diagnosis of chronic HCV infection.
Exclusion: Has a history of stroke.
Exclusion: Has a history of chronic seizures.
Exclusion: Has a history of cancer.
Exclusion: Has a history of human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection.
Exclusion: Has had major surgery within the past 3 months.
Exclusion: Has donated blood within the past 3 months.
Exclusion: Has participated in another investigational study within the past 3 months.
